1.name: Beneficiary Sharing Network
1.description: Customer shares beneficiaries with confirmed fraud accounts. Signals include shared beneficiary count and known-bad payee overlap.
1.source: CIP 05 — Beneficiary Breakdown · Beneficiary Network Detection (Customer Level v2) · Suspected Client Detection — Beneficiary Sharing Network
1.analyst_guidance: Review the shared beneficiary/account, relationship rationale, transaction direction, and whether the same payee appears across unrelated customers.

2.name: WPS Salary Irregularity
2.description: Employer mismatch between declared employer and WPS disbursement, abnormal salary frequency, or ghost-employer signal.
2.source: CIP 06 — WPS Salary Records · WPS Salary Verification — Customer Enrichment · WPS Incoming Salary — Payment Frequency Bucket Analysis
2.analyst_guidance: Compare declared employer, WPS sender, salary cadence, and whether salary patterns support the customer profile.

3.name: Feedzai Fraud Signals (90d)
3.description: Recent high-risk Feedzai score, alerts, blocks, risk classification, or confirmed fraud event on transfer/card activity.
3.source: CIP 04 — Fraud Signals (Last 90 Days) · CIP 10 — Fraud Alerts & Confirmed Fraud
3.analyst_guidance: Check alert history, blocked events, confirmed fraud decisions, and whether recent alerts align with the network path.

4.name: Mule Account Signals
4.description: Composite mule signal using flow-through percentage, rapid drain, beneficiary velocity, sender count, and transaction velocity.
4.source: Card 12 — Mule Detection Score (Dashboard) · Mule Account Detection — Retail + SME
4.analyst_guidance: Prioritise transaction timeline review, inbound/outbound sequencing, cash-out paths, and rapid onward movement.

5.name: Related Account Fraud Flags
5.description: Customer is connected through Emirates ID or relationship graph to another account with fraud indicators.
5.source: SME Network Expansion — Full Fraud Investigation Enrichment v2.7 · Retail Customers Information (same-Emirates-ID accounts)
5.analyst_guidance: Review the relationship path and decide whether the connected account risk should influence this customer's investigation.

6.name: Employer Blacklist Association
6.description: Customer employer appears in an off-boarded or fraud-linked employer list, or has fraud-linked peers from the same employer.
6.source: CIP 06 — WPS Salary Records · Retail Customers Information (employer grouping)
6.analyst_guidance: Check employer name, employee cluster, offboarding history, and whether the employer relationship is legitimate.

7.name: Device & Contact Sharing
7.description: Device ID, phone number, or email is shared across multiple accounts, especially with rejected or fraud-actioned customers.
7.source: Retail Fraud Detection — Device & Identity Sharing Analysis · Card 07 — Device & Login Intelligence (Dashboard)
7.analyst_guidance: Review shared device/contact footprint, login activity, and whether accounts appear controlled by the same actor.

8.name: Rejected Applicant Contact Overlap
8.description: Customer phone or email was previously used in a rejected onboarding application.
8.source: Retail Fraud Detection — Device & Identity Sharing Analysis · CIP 01 (Identity & Contact, rejection fields from retail_dim_customer)
8.analyst_guidance: Review rejected applicant history, shared phone/email, and whether this account bypassed earlier onboarding concerns.

9.name: Support Ticket Irregularities
9.description: Fraud-related support or operational tickets, including account closure requests, fraud recalls, or suspicious activity reports.
9.source: Card 09 — Freshworks Support Tickets · Fraud Account Closure — Offboarding Reason Categorisation · Backoffice Cases — Fraud Recall Filter
9.analyst_guidance: Check ticket chronology, complaint type, recall references, and whether tickets align with transaction/network evidence.

10.name: Transaction Behaviour (90d)
10.description: Behavioural anomaly based on counterparty count, average transaction size, active days, inbound/outbound ratio, or flow-through percentage.
10.source: Card 03 — Txn Summary (90d) · Wio Accounts Transaction History
10.analyst_guidance: Use as supporting context; confirm with transaction timelines and customer profile before escalating.

11.name: Back Office Case History
11.description: Existing back office cases flagged as fraud recall or AML, including presence and count of prior cases.
11.source: Card 13 — Back Office Cases · Backoffice Cases — Fraud Recall Filter
11.analyst_guidance: Review previous case outcomes and avoid duplicating already-resolved concerns unless new evidence is present.

12.name: Card Fraud Events
12.description: Card-level fraud events, alert rate, fraud decisions, or confirmed card fraud indicators.
12.source: Card Fraud Investigation · CIP 10 — Fraud Alerts & Confirmed Fraud
12.analyst_guidance: Check whether card fraud is linked to account takeover, mule behaviour, or unrelated card misuse.

risk_overlay_metadata.pii_status: Uses only anonymized CUSTOMER_XXX / EID_XXX / ACCOUNT_XXX aliases from demo JSON. No raw identifiers added.
risk_overlay_metadata.generation_note: Synthetic fraud indicators were assigned deterministically for demo purposes using graph context such as seed status, shared EID links, shared counterparty accounts, transaction counts, amount, and account policy.
